sanskrit:
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते॥
transliteration: mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣayoḥ | sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Equal in honor and dishonor, the same toward friend and foe, having renounced all undertakings—such a one is said to have crossed beyond the guṇas.
contextual_meaning: The psyche that no longer inflates or deflates with social feedback, that no longer splits the world into allies or threats, and that has released compulsive doing, has stabilized in a post-egoic equilibrium.
psychological_state: Neuroceptive safety: the nervous system no longer registers social evaluation as survival data; self-worth is internally referenced, producing affective flat-line toward praise or insult.
trigger_scenario: Your post goes viral—half call you a hero, half a fraud. Notifications explode. You notice the pulse rise, then watch it drop back to baseline as you close the app and make tea, untouched by either adulation or outrage.
anchor_text: Rest untroubled by praise or blame, friend or foe, success or failure.
